_meta.notes: Post-injection body duration research for catalog compounds. halfLifeHours = terminal elimination half-life when available; effectiveDurationHours = practical duration of action. certainty: ESTABLISHED (human label/trial PK), ESTIMATED (limited human or solid preclinical), UNCERTAIN (sparse sources). Citations merge into catalog Citation rows via dosing fixtures.

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Plasma half-life in animals is short (minutes); tissue effects may last hours. Supports once- or twice-daily dosing in research protocols rather than weekly—human SC PK is not established.

citations.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Native thymosin β4 plasma half-life is on the order of ~2 h IV; tissue distribution and actin-binding can prolong local effects. Community twice-weekly loading is not driven by multi-day plasma t½—human SC PK for TB-500 is limited.

citations.title: Thymosin beta-4: a multi-functional regenerative peptide. Basic properties and clinical applications

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Terminal t½ ≈1 week after SC injection (Ozempic/Wegovy labels) supports once-weekly dosing on the same day each week.

citations.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity
Ozempic (semaglutide) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Mean t½ ≈5 days after SC supports once-weekly dosing (Mounjaro/Zepbound labels).

citations.title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity
Mounjaro (tirzepatide) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Copper-tripeptide plasma clearance is rapid; community injectable protocols use once-daily dosing. Robust human injectable PK is lacking—topical cosmetic kinetics differ from SC mg charts.

citations.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of the GHK-Cu Peptide in the Light of the New Gene Data

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Egrifta label: mean SC t½ ≈26–38 minutes. Short systemic exposure drives once-daily (not weekly) dosing; GH pulse is the therapeutic signal, not multi-day plasma levels.

citations.title: Egrifta (tesamorelin) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Short-lived tetrapeptide; no validated human terminal t½. Research/community regimens use daily or multi-day courses rather than weekly depot-style dosing.

citations.title: Peptide bioregulator Epitalon: experimental and clinical studies (review context for short-acting pineal peptides)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Short tripeptide bioregulators clear quickly; daily multi-day courses are typical. Human PK after injection is not established.

citations.title: Pinealon and short peptide bioregulators: experimental literature (human injectable PK not established)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Mitochondrial-derived peptide; animal work implies short plasma residence with cellular signaling effects. Community charts often use daily SC—human half-life after injection is not well defined.

citations.title: The mitochondrial-derived peptide MOTS-c promotes metabolic homeostasis and reduces obesity and insulin resistance

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: α-MSH tripeptide fragment; expected short plasma t½. Supports once- or twice-daily research dosing rather than weekly schedules—human SC PK sparse.

citations.title: Anti-inflammatory effects of α-MSH and KPV peptide (preclinical; human injectable PK limited)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Cibinetide (ARA-290) clinical programs used frequent (often daily or multi-times-per-week) SC/IV dosing consistent with short-to-moderate half-life—not a weekly GLP-1-like depot. Exact SC t½ varies by study; mark as estimated.

citations.title: ARA290, a non-erythropoietic peptide engineered from erythropoietin, improves metabolic control and neuropathic symptoms in patients with type 2 diabetes

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Both cagrilintide (long-acting amylin analog, multi-day t½) and semaglutide (~1 week t½) support once-weekly co-administration in clinical development.

citations.title: Cagrilintide plus semaglutide for weight management (REDEFINE / co-admin programs)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Triple agonist clinical PK supports roughly multi-day to ~1-week terminal half-life and once-weekly SC dosing in phase 2 obesity trials.

citations.title: Triple–Hormone-Receptor Agonist Retatrutide for Obesity — A Phase 2 Trial

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Zadaxin/thymalfasin literature reports short SC plasma half-life (order of hours); approved/clinical schedules are multi-times weekly (e.g. BIW), not once weekly long-acting GLP-1 style.

citations.title: Thymosin alpha 1: a comprehensive review of the literature

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Blend (typically GHK-Cu / BPC-157 / TB-500 components): shortest plasma half-lives dominate systemic exposure; frequency follows daily-to-several-times-weekly community charts for components, not a single long-acting depot.

citations.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: No published human PK. Preclinical FOXO4-DRI work used intermittent dosing; any half-life figure is a rough research estimate only—do not treat as clinical certainty.

citations.title: Targeted Apoptosis of Senescent Cells Restores Tissue Homeostasis in Response to Chemotoxicity and Aging

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Delta-sleep-inducing peptide has a very short plasma half-life (minutes in experimental literature). Nightly or as-needed dosing targets acute sleep effects, not multi-day plasma coverage.

citations.title: Delta-sleep-inducing peptide (DSIP): a review of experimental data

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: GHRP-family PK: hexarelin half-life commonly cited ~55–90+ minutes. Supports multi-times-daily GH pulse protocols rather than weekly dosing.

citations.title: Hexarelin, a synthetic growth-hormone releasing peptide: pharmacokinetics and GH response

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Prohibitin-targeting peptide (TP-01/FTPP); primate studies used multi-day courses. Human PK after injection is not established—frequency remains experimental.

citations.title: A peptidomimetic targeting white fat causes weight loss and improved insulin resistance in obese monkeys

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Unesterified / aqueous testosterone is cleared rapidly; clinical replacement uses esterified depot forms. Catalog 'Testosterone' without ester should not be dosed like cypionate weekly—short effective duration implies frequent or different formulation.

citations.title: Testosterone therapy in men with hypogonadism: an Endocrine Society clinical practice guideline

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: IM testosterone cypionate terminal t½ commonly ~8 days; labels and guidelines support weekly or every-1–2-week injection schedules for replacement.

citations.title: Depo-Testosterone (testosterone cypionate) prescribing information

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Oral Cialis label: mean t½ ≈17.5 h supports once-daily low-dose or as-needed (effects lasting up to ~36 h). Not primarily an injectable peptide, but duration still drives daily vs PRN frequency.

citations.title: Cialis (tadalafil) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Levitra label: t½ ≈4–5 h → as-needed dosing before sexual activity, not continuous weekly coverage.

citations.title: Levitra (vardenafil) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Thymic peptide complex used in short daily courses in Russian clinical literature; human terminal t½ poorly characterized in Western PK—daily multi-day blocks, not weekly long-acting dosing.

citations.title: Thymalin in the regulation of immunity and hemostasis (review of clinical experience)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Oral chlorodehydromethyltestosterone active life commonly cited ~16 h; supports once- or split-daily oral schedules (not an injectable peptide depot).

citations.title: Anabolic-androgenic steroid pharmacokinetics overview (oral 4-chlorodehydromethyltestosterone)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Oral methandrostenolone short half-life (~3–5 h) historically led to multi-times-daily divided dosing rather than once-weekly injection schedules.

citations.title: Pharmacology of anabolic steroids

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: N-acetyl/amidate Selank analogs are designed for improved stability vs native Selank, but still support daily (often multi-dose or intranasal) schedules—not weekly SC depot PK.

citations.title: Selank and its analogs: experimental pharmacology (stability-modified forms)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: hCG after IM/SC has multi-hour to ~1–1.5 day disposition; hypogonadism and fertility protocols commonly dose several times per week rather than daily microdosing or weekly ultra-long analogs.

citations.title: Pregnyl (chorionic gonadotropin) prescribing information

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Menotropins contain FSH (longer elimination) and LH (shorter). Fertility protocols use daily or near-daily injections—component half-lives differ; treat range as estimated composite.

citations.title: Menopur (menotropins) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Cathelicidin peptide; systemic half-life short in experimental settings. Research use often daily or short courses—human SC PK incomplete.

citations.title: The human cathelicidin LL-37 — a multifunctional peptide (review)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Tuftsin analog with short plasma residence (minutes in experimental data); clinical/intranasal schedules use multi-times daily or short courses, not weekly injection.

citations.title: Selank: experimental and clinical studies of anxiolytic peptide

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: GHRH(1-29) t½ typically ~10–20 minutes SC/IV; once-daily (often bedtime) dosing aims for physiologic GH pulses, not continuous plasma coverage.

citations.title: Sermorelin: a review of its use in diagnosis and treatment of children with idiopathic GH deficiency

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Acetyl octapeptide-3 is primarily a topical cosmetic peptide; systemic injectable PK is not clinically established. Topical use is typically daily—do not infer weekly SC depot kinetics.

citations.title: A synthetic hexapeptide (Argireline) with antiwrinkle activity (related SNAP-25 pathway peptides)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Somatropin SC t½ ~2–3.5 h (product labels); IGF-1 rises longer. Daily (or 6–7×/week) injection is standard—not weekly like some long-acting GH analogs.

citations.title: Genotropin (somatropin) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: ACTH(4-10) analog with short plasma life; Russian clinical use is multi-times daily (often intranasal). Injection would still imply frequent dosing, not weekly.

citations.title: Semax, an ACTH(4-10) analogue with nootropic properties

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: N-acetyl/amidate Semax aims for greater stability than native Semax, still consistent with daily multi-dose regimens rather than long-acting weekly PK.

citations.title: Semax and modified analogs: experimental neuroprotection literature

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Exogenous NAD+/NAD precursors show rapid disposition; IV infusions are timed events, SC research use is typically daily or multi-times weekly—not a week-long plasma depot.

citations.title: NAD+ metabolism and therapeutic implications (review of pharmacokinetics challenges)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Pitocin label: plasma t½ ~1–6 minutes IV (context-dependent). Clinical effects require continuous infusion or frequent redosing—not weekly SC.

citations.title: Pitocin (oxytocin) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: GHRP-2 half-life reported ~30–35 minutes in clinical PK work; multi-times-daily dosing produces discrete GH pulses.

citations.title: Pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of GHRP-2 in humans

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: GHRP-6 short half-life (tens of minutes) drives multi-times-daily pulse protocols, often stacked with a GHRH analog.

citations.title: Growth hormone-releasing peptide-6: pharmacokinetics and endocrine effects

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Clinical PK reports ~2 h half-life; commonly 1–3× daily with a short-acting GHRH analog (e.g. CJC no DAC), not weekly.

citations.title: Ipamorelin, a novel selective growth hormone secretagogue: first human dose trial

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Long-acting amylin analog engineered for multi-day half-life; clinical development uses once-weekly SC dosing (alone or with semaglutide).

citations.title: Once-weekly cagrilintide for weight management: phase 2 trial

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Higher-dose GLOW-style blend of short-to-moderate lived research peptides; dose frequency should follow the shortest-lived active components (daily-to-several-times-weekly), not a single weekly depot half-life.

citations.title: Thymosin beta-4: a multi-functional regenerative peptide

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Mod GRF 1-29 (CJC without DAC) t½ ~30–60 minutes—unlike DAC form (days). Supports multi-times-daily or bedtime pulse dosing with a GHRP, not weekly.

citations.title: CJC-1295, a long-acting GHRH analog: distinction of DAC vs non-DAC (Mod GRF) pharmacokinetics

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Both components are short-acting (CJC no DAC ~30–60 min; ipamorelin ~2 h). Stack is typically 1–3× daily for GH pulses—body duration of the blend is limited by the shorter peptide.

citations.title: Ipamorelin, a novel selective growth hormone secretagogue: first human dose trial
CJC-1295 DAC vs non-DAC pharmacokinetics context

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Blend of two research peptides with short-to-moderate systemic half-lives; community stacks often daily BPC with less frequent TB-500. No single validated human t½ for the combination product.

citations.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing
Thymosin beta-4 regenerative peptide review

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: hGH fragment 176–191 analog; clinical and community use has been once-daily. Human terminal t½ after SC is not as well documented as full-length somatropin—treat as uncertain short-acting.

citations.title: Safety and tolerability of the hexadecapeptide AOD9604 in humans

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Long R3 IGF-1 is engineered for longer half-life than native IGF-1 (~minutes–hours → roughly ~20–30 h class estimates). Supports once-daily research dosing more than multi-hourly native IGF-1; human SC PK still limited.

citations.title: Long R3 insulin-like growth factor-I: biological properties and therapeutic potential (review/context)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Afamelanotide SC solution is short-lived vs the approved SC implant (Scenesse, months-long release). DIY multi-times-weekly charts reflect non-depot kinetics; implant PK does not transfer to simple SC injections.

citations.title: Scenesse (afamelanotide) implant prescribing information — contrast to non-depot SC use

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Spadin/TREK-1 pathway peptide fragment; human injectable PK unpublished. Preclinical dosing was frequent—assume short-acting and daily research schedules with explicit uncertainty.

citations.title: Spadin, a sortilin-derived peptide: antidepressant properties (related PE-22-28 research lineage)

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Elamipretide (SS-31) clinical programs used daily SC or IV regimens consistent with hours-scale half-life—not weekly long-acting.

citations.title: Elamipretide (SS-31) clinical development and pharmacokinetics overview

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Active site fragment of thymosin β4; expected short-to-moderate systemic half-life similar to parent peptide class. Human PK sparse—research frequency often multi-times weekly rather than proven weekly depot.

citations.title: Thymosin beta-4: a multi-functional regenerative peptide

bodyDuration.frequencyImplication: Vyleesi (bremelanotide) label: mean terminal t½ ≈2.7 h after SC. Dosed as needed before anticipated sexual activity (max frequency limits on label)—not chronic weekly therapy.

citations.title: Vyleesi (bremelanotide) prescribing information — pharmacokinetics
Bremelanotide for the Treatment of Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder: Two Randomized Phase 3 Trials
